title: Check Point Harmony SASE Threat Prevention (Check Point)
dataSources: `sourcetype=cp_log` (Check Point Log Exporter), Smart-1 Cloud logs
app: Check Point App for Splunk (Splunkbase 4293), CCX Add-on for Checkpoint Smart-1 Cloud (Splunkbase 7259)
spl:
index=checkpoint sourcetype="cp_log" earliest=-24h
| where match(lower(product),"(?i)ips|anti.?bot|anti.?malware|anti.?virus|threat.?emulation|threat.?extraction")
| stats count by protection_name, severity, src, action
| sort -count
description: Check Point Harmony SASE applies ThreatCloud AI-powered prevention (IPS, anti-malware, anti-bot, threat emulation) to all user traffic via cloud enforcement points. Detection events reveal threats targeting users regardless of location.
value: Security operations teams analyze Check Point Harmony SASE threat prevention across all blades (Anti-Bot, Anti-Virus, IPS, Threat Emulation), prioritizing Anti-Bot detections as indicators of compromised endpoints and Threat Emulation hits as zero-day catches worth extracting IOCs from.
implementation: Configure Check Point Log Exporter or Smart-1 Cloud syslog forwarding to Splunk. Map `protection_name` to ThreatCloud categories. Alert on critical-severity detections. Correlate with MITRE ATT&CK via Check Point attack IDs.
visualization: Bar chart (detections by protection type), Table (top threats), Timeline (detection events), Pie chart (severity distribution).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count

references.title: CCX Add-on for Checkpoint Smart-1 Cloud

knownFalsePositives: Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Check Point Harmony SASE (formerly Perimeter 81) threat events in `index=checkpoint` with `sourcetype=checkpoint:harmony`. Check Point logs via Log Exporter or Open Telemetry Collector. Key fields: `blade` (Anti-Bot/Anti-Virus/IPS/Threat Emulation), `action` (Prevent/Detect/Drop), `malware_name`, `severity`, `src`, `dst`, `service_id`, `user`, `confidence_level`.
• Harmony SASE includes Check Point's ThreatCloud intelligence with SandBlast threat emulation (sandboxing) for zero-day prevention. Events span Anti-Bot, Anti-Virus, IPS, and Threat Emulation blades.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Configure Check Point Log Exporter: SmartConsole > Logs > Log Exporter > add Splunk destination. Or use OPSEC LEA.

Verify:
```spl
index=checkpoint sourcetype="checkpoint:harmony" earliest=-7d
| stats count by blade, action
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Threat prevention by blade:
```spl
index=checkpoint sourcetype="checkpoint:harmony" earliest=-24h
| stats count dc(src) as sources dc(dst) as targets by blade, action, severity
| sort blade, severity, - count
```

Understanding this SPL: Shows threat detections across all Check Point blades. Anti-Bot detections mean infected endpoints calling C2. Anti-Virus catches known malware. IPS blocks known exploits. Threat Emulation (sandboxing) catches zero-day threats not seen before — these are the highest value detections.

Threat Emulation (zero-day) detections:
```spl
index=checkpoint sourcetype="checkpoint:harmony" blade="Threat Emulation" earliest=-7d
| stats count by malware_name, confidence_level, action
| sort - count
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Download EICAR test file. Verify Anti-Virus detection.
(b) In SmartConsole: Logs & Monitor > Threat Prevention.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — Blade detection distribution. Row 2 — Zero-day (Threat Emulation) hits. Row 3 — Anti-Bot C2 callbacks.
Alert: Immediate on Anti-Bot detections (compromised endpoint). Immediate on Threat Emulation with high confidence.

Runbook:
1. **Anti-Bot detection** — Internal host compromised. Isolate and investigate.
2. **Threat Emulation hit** — Zero-day caught. Extract IOCs and share with threat team.
3. **IPS high severity** — Known exploit. Verify patch status of target.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **No Threat Emulation events** — SandBlast license may be required.
• **All 'Detect' no 'Prevent'** — Blades may be in detect-only mode.
grandmaExplanation: Check Point's security catches threats at multiple levels — known viruses, exploit attempts, botnet communications, and even brand-new attacks it has never seen before (by running suspicious files in a sandbox). We track each type to know which defenses are catching what.
hardware: Check Point Harmony SASE (cloud), Harmony Endpoint (client), Smart-1 Cloud (management)
